summary: McKenzie briefly updates on setting up his eSellerate account for selling software. He finds the mechanics of listing a product straightforward, estimating he could do it in under 30 minutes. However, he criticizes eSellerate's checkout flow for requiring too many page clicks, noting this likely hurts conversion rates. The main customization challenge is skinning the checkout to match his website's look and feel.
key_points: eSellerate's product listing process is smooth and can be completed in under 30 minutes
Skinning the checkout to match your website's branding is the main customization challenge
The multi-step checkout flow (minimum 5 pages) is a serious conversion rate concern
money_quotes: The actual mechanics of getting the product up and running are smooth as silk, and I estimate that I have played around with them for about 4-5 hours and could get a product listed in under 30 minutes if I really wanted to, start to finish.
My one complain about eSellerate — the number of pages you have to click through just screams "Abandon this software purchase!".
The lowest I've been able to force it to is about 5 steps. Yeah, egads.
key_insight: Even when a payment platform's setup is easy, a lengthy checkout flow with too many steps actively drives customers away from completing purchases.
